source.title: Role of the extended amygdala in short-duration versus sustained fear: a tribute to Dr. Lennart Heimer.

confidence_rationale: Walker and Davis explicitly dissect CeA(M) as the rapid PHASIC fear output channel, contrasted with BNST sustained fear. This directly grounds MECH-074c's fast_prime in the CeA(M)->hypothalamus/brainstem axis. The phasic/sustained dissociation validates MECH-074c's claim that fast_prime is distinct from MECH-046's slower cortical mode-prior write.
failure_signatures: Phasic/sustained dissociation based on stimulus duration manipulation, not direct timing measurements at millisecond scale relevant to 1-2 sim steps.
CRF-mediated BLA-to-BNST pathway for sustained fear creates a dual-output architecture that may require separate treatment in REE (not currently modelled).

mapping.source_claim_statement: CeA(M) activates hypothalamus and brainstem targets to produce rapid, short-duration (phasic) fear responses. BNST(L) produces slower, sustained fear/anxiety. BLA activates CeA(M) for immediate responses and CeA(L)/BNST for sustained responses. CeA(L) releases CRF into BNST(L) to drive sustained response.
mapping.ree_translation: CeA(M) phasic output is the biological grounding for MECH-074c's fast_prime: a rapid, decaying signal that biases the harm action channel within 1-2 sim steps. The BNST sustained pathway maps conceptually to MECH-046's slower cortical mode-prior write, validating the architectural distinction MECH-074c draws.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Phasic vs. sustained here refers to seconds-to-minutes, not 100-200 ms. The 1-2 sim-step specification of fast_prime is grounded in Mendez-Bertolo 2016 (amygdala LFP during visual threat at 74 ms), which is the latency-specific evidence.
mapping.source_context: Rodent fear-potentiated startle paradigm, CeA vs. BNST lesion studies, conditioned stimulus duration manipulation.

confidence_components.notes: Davis lab, highly cited review; temporal resolution of phasic/sustained framing is at seconds scale, not milliseconds.
